data.description: PEPE's latest price analysis reveals a significant increase of 15% on September 6, 2025.

data.title: Apple faces class-action lawsuit for using copyrighted books to train AI, accused of using pirated books; complaint filed by authors in California court.
data.description: Apple Inc. is facing a class-action lawsuit alleging that it used copyrighted books without permission to train its AI systems. Authors Grady Hendrix and Jennifer Roberson filed the complaint in a California federal court, accusing Apple of copying protected works without consent, credit, or compensation. The lawsuit targets Apple's "OpenELM" large language models, claiming the use of a dataset of pirated books. Both authors found their works in the unauthorized training materials. This is part of a broader trend of legal challenges in the tech industry over AI training practices. Microsoft Corp. has also faced similar claims. The current stock price of AAPL can be found through the NASDAQ exchange.

data.title: Trading debut of World Liberty Financial (WLFI) token falls flat, compared to successful Trump family meme coins reaching $75B valuation.
data.description: The introduction of the WLFI token on the trading market has caused a stir, shining a spotlight on other cryptocurrencies linked to the Trump family. President Donald Trump and First Lady Melania Trump released their official meme coins in January, just before the presidential inauguration. Both MELANIA and TRUMP experienced a surge in value after their launch, with the combined market worth surpassing that of Occidental Petroleum Corp (NYSE: OXY). TRUMP, in particular, attained a fully diluted value of close to $75 billion within two days, a milestone that typically takes numerous publicly traded companies years to reach. Nonetheless, the excitement was short-lived as TRUMP hit a peak of $75.35 before dropping rapidly. The value of the coin continued to decrease over time, and currently, it is trading 89% below its all-time high, leading to a significant collapse in its fully diluted valuation. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: 22nd Century Group to participate in HC Wainwright Conference in NYC on Sep 9, 2025. CEO and CFO to host investor meetings.
data.description: In Mocksville, North Carolina on September 5, 2025, 22nd Century Group, Inc. (Nasdaq: XXII) disclosed its ongoing efforts for the past 27 years to combat the dangers of smoking stemming from nicotine addiction. The company shared that Larry Firestone, CEO, and Dan Otto, CFO, will be conducting investor meetings on September 9, 2025. These meetings are scheduled as part of the HC Wainwright Conference happening at the Lotte New York Palace Hotel.

data.title: RYBREVANT® + LAZCLUZE® prevent resistance vs osimertinib in lung cancer. Combination extends survival, reduces mutations. #JNJ #MARIPOSA
data.description: Today in Barcelona, Spain, Johnson & Johnson (NYSE: JNJ) revealed that RYBREVANT®, a combination therapy, has been shown to prolong survival and notably decrease prevalent EGFR and MET resistance mutations typically found in osimertinib-based treatments. The data stems from recent analyses conducted on the Phase 3 MARIPOSA study. This development offers promising results in the fight against cancers with these specific mutations, marking a significant advancement in treatment efficacy and patient outcomes.

data.title: Brazil's biggest asset manager Itaú Asset creates dedicated crypto unit within its billion-dollar mutual funds arm to generate alpha for clients.
data.description: Itaú Asset is introducing a new crypto department within its mutual funds division valued at billions of dollars. The goal is to provide enhanced returns for customers through trading digital assets.

data.title: Chewy (NYSE: CHWY) expected to report Q2 fiscal 2025 earnings of $1.41/share on revenue of $3.08 billion. Holds 33% of online pet market share, with 20M active customers.
data.description: Chewy, Inc. (NYSE: CHWY) is set to release its Q2 2025 financial results on September 10. Analysts, as per Benzinga Pro data, are anticipating adjusted earnings of $1.41 per share with revenues reaching $3.08 billion. The online pet retailer boasts a customer base of over 20 million and maintains a prominent position in the digital transition of the market. With online penetration standing at 38% for the U.S. pet sector in fiscal 2024, there is significant growth potential, of which Chewy holds about a 33% share. JPMorgan expresses optimism, indicating further room for growth in the sector. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: OP expected to test $0.88 resistance before dropping to $0.54 by October 2025. Technical analysis indicates potential movement.
data.description: According to technical analysis, there is a potential for OP to increase towards a resistance level of $0.88 in the upcoming weeks. However, after this bullish movement, it is expected that bearish momentum will cause the price to decrease towards a target of $0.54 by October 2025.

data.title: Nvidia warns new bill could hinder global competition, clashes with Trump's China chip export deal. AI GAIN Act requires prioritizing domestic orders for chips with processing power of 4,800+. Nvidia disputes need for restrictions.
data.description: NVIDIA Corp (NVDA) issued a warning that impending Congressional legislation might impact its global competitiveness, causing tensions following President Trump's recent chip sales agreement with China. The proposed Guaranteeing Access and Innovation for National Artificial Intelligence Act, a part of the National Defense Authorization Act, necessitates AI chipmakers to prioritize domestic orders for advanced processors before fulfilling foreign requests. The legislation also demands the Commerce Department to reject export licenses for powerful AI chips with a processing power of 4,800 or more while U.S. entities remain pending for chip deliveries. Despite this, Nvidia disputes the necessity for such restrictions, emphasizing its commitment to servicing American customers. For further details, refer to the original report.

data.title: Economists defend job data reliability amid skepticism after Trump fired BLS head.
data.description: The recent dismissal of the head of the Bureau of Labor Statistics by President Trump has sparked concerns over potential political meddling. This move has raised questions about the independence of the agency responsible for key economic data and analysis. The decision comes amidst heightened scrutiny of government transparency and accountability. The Bureau of Labor Statistics plays a crucial role in providing important labor market information, including unemployment rates and job growth figures. President Trump's action has prompted criticism from various quarters, with many calling for a transparent and impartial process in selecting leadership for such vital agencies. The move has also drawn attention to the potential impact on the credibility and integrity of labor market data during this period of economic uncertainty.

data.title: Northland Power retires John Brace from Board, appoints Sébastien Clerc; changes effective Sept 5, 2025 (TSX: NPI).
data.description: Toronto-based company Northland Power Inc. (TSX: NPI) shared updates on its Board of Directors, with John Brace retiring and Sébastien Clerc appointed on September 5, 2025. This change signifies a shift in leadership with Clerc joining the Board alongside the departure of Brace.

data.title: Bitcoin's price surged, boosting altcoins like ETH, BNB, and SOL. Altseason may be on the horizon. #cryptocurrency #pricepredictions
data.description: The price of Bitcoin has surged towards its upper range limits, signaling a potential breakout and leading to positive momentum for various alternative cryptocurrencies. This increase has sparked speculation about the possibility of an 'altseason' in the crypto market, as investors eye the rising value and potential for growth in these alternative assets. The upward movement in Bitcoin, approaching key resistance levels, has created a bullish sentiment among traders, with many closely monitoring price movements and market indicators for potential opportunities. This development comes amid increasing interest and investment in digital assets, with market participants closely watching the performance of Bitcoin and altcoins as key indicators of market trends.

data.title: G-III Apparel beats Q2 estimates with 25 cents EPS on $613.266 million sales but reduces FY adjusted EPS outlook to $2.55-$2.75 from $4.15-$4.25 and FY sales forecast to $3.02 billion from $3.14 billion.
data.description: G-III Apparel Group (NASDAQ: GIII) exceeded expectations in the second quarter with adjusted earnings per share of 25 cents, surpassing the estimated 9 cents. Despite reporting quarterly sales of $613.266 million, which beat projections of $571.312 million, there was a 5% year-over-year decrease. However, the company reduced its fiscal 2026 outlook, adjusting the EPS forecast to $2.55–$2.75 from $4.15–$4.25, falling below the estimated $2.90. Similarly, the sales forecast was lowered to $3.02 billion from $3.14 billion. The revised sales guidance is slightly under the $3.131 billion consensus.

data.title: Chainlink's price predicted to reach $24.59 with resistance at $27.87 and support at $22.04. Mixed outlook.
data.description: The predicted price for LINK indicates a potential increase to $24.59 in the upcoming weeks despite the ongoing bearish trend. The key resistance level stands at $27.87, while the support level is at $22.04, crucial in determining the future direction of the price.

data.title: Sen. Warren blasts Trump for broken promises, accuses him of raising costs; Trump family reportedly gains $5 billion in crypto venture.
data.description: On Friday, Senator Elizabeth Warren criticized President Donald Trump for failing to deliver on promises to reduce costs, accusing him of increasing expenses for Americans. Warren accused Trump of being dishonest and pointed to rising prices for groceries, gas, school supplies, and baby strollers. She stated that instead of lowering grocery expenses, Trump's tariff policies have raised costs for consumers. Additionally, Warren mentioned that the Trump family's investment in World Liberty Financial's WLFI token reportedly increased their wealth by approximately $5 billion following its trading launch.

data.title: Rackspace Technology granted inducement equity awards to new CEO Gajen Kandiah on Sept 4, 2025 under Nasdaq rule.
data.description: On September 4, 2025, Rackspace Technology®, a leading provider of hybrid cloud and AI solutions, disclosed that they awarded inducement equity awards to Gajen Kandiah upon his appointment as the new Chief Executive Officer. The awards were granted as part of his recruitment package and reflect the company's confidence in his leadership. Rackspace Technology® (NASDAQ: RXT) continues to strengthen its team with key individuals, reinforcing its position in the market.

data.title: Litecoin clashes with influencer in a war of words over price movements and personal appearance.
data.description: Litecoin responded to Benjamin Cowen's criticism of its price performance by humorously poking fun at his hairline, teasing, "It reminds us of the great recession." The incident garnered attention as they engaged in banter over cryptocurrency with many viewers. Reactions were mixed to this exchange, with some finding it amusing while others thought it was unprofessional. The incident achieved significant social media engagement, with the video receiving over 10,000 views and sparking discussions among the crypto community. This playful interaction highlights the dynamic and sometimes lighthearted nature of online discourse surrounding cryptocurrencies.

data.title: Toro posts Q3 results with net sales of $1.13 billion, net earnings of $53.5 million, adjusted earnings rise to $122.5 million.
data.description: In the fiscal third quarter, The Toro Company (NYSE: TTC) reported net sales of $1.13 billion, showing a 2% decrease from the previous year and falling short of the analyst estimate of $1.16 billion. Net earnings dropped to $53.5 million from $119.3 million, resulting in diluted earnings per share of 54 cents. This included a noncash impairment charge of 62 cents per share, equating to $81 million pretax. Adjusted net earnings were $122.5 million, slightly lower than the $123.7 million from a year ago; however, adjusted diluted earnings per share rose by 5% to $1.24, surpassing the $1.22 estimate. The company exceeded expectations with their third-quarter adjusted earnings, particularly within their Professional segment.

data.title: Midea Group introduces SPACE MASTER appliances at IFA 2025, focusing on space efficiency with "Same Size, Big Surprise" concept. #Midea #IFA2025
data.description: On September 6, 2025, in Berlin, the Midea Group, a Fortune Global 500 company for 2025, showcased its SPACE MASTER appliances at IFA 2025. The series, emphasizing "Same Size, Big Surprise," revolutionizes the amount of usable space offered to users.

data.title: Labor market sluggish in summer. August sees addition of 22K jobs. Revised data shows 13K job loss in June, first since Dec 2020.
data.description: In August, employers increased their workforce by 22,000 jobs. Additionally, revised data revealed that in June, there was a decrease of 13,000 jobs, marking the initial overall loss in employment since December 2020.
